title: DPIIT Startup India Recognition
tagline: Get your official government recognition — the single most impactful step for any Indian startup.
unlocks: Income tax exemption for 3 consecutive years (Section 80-IAC)
Exemption from Angel Tax (Section 56(2)(viib)) via Form 2
80% rebate on patent filing fees + fast-track examination
Self-certification under 9 labour laws and 3 environment laws
Access to Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (up to ₹50 lakh)
Access to Fund of Funds for Startups (SIDBI-managed VC funds)
Credit Guarantee Scheme for Startups (CGSS) — collateral-free loans up to ₹10 crore
GeM Startup Runway — sell to government without prior turnover requirement
Startup India Learning Program (free courses + resources)

steps.title: Check Eligibility
steps.description: Verify your startup meets DPIIT's eligibility criteria before applying.
details: Entity age: Incorporated/registered less than 10 years ago
Annual turnover: Less than ₹100 crore in any previous financial year
Innovation: Working towards development, improvement, or commercialisation of a new product/process/service OR using technology in a scalable business model
Entity type: Private Limited Company, Limited Liability Partnership (LLP), or Registered Partnership Firm
Not formed by splitting or restructuring an existing business
tips: Even if you're pre-revenue or at idea stage, you can apply — 'innovation' is broadly interpreted
Holding companies or subsidiaries of large corporates do not qualify

steps.title: Incorporate Your Entity
steps.description: Register as a Private Limited Company, LLP, or Partnership Firm with MCA.
details: Private Limited Company: Register on MCA21 portal (mca.gov.in) via SPICe+ form
LLP: Register on MCA21 via FiLLiP form
Partnership Firm: Register under Indian Partnership Act with your state Registrar of Firms
Obtain Certificate of Incorporation (CoI) from MCA — this is your primary document
Get PAN and TAN for the entity from Income Tax department
Open a current account in the entity's name
documents_needed: Proposed company name (reserve via RUN form)
tips: Private Limited Company is strongly preferred over LLP if you plan to raise VC funding (FEMA compliance is simpler)
Use a CA or company secretary to incorporate — cost ₹5,000–₹15,000 and saves errors
Minimum authorized capital for Pvt Ltd: ₹1 lakh (can be higher)
Registered office can be your home address — just provide utility bill proof

steps.title: Register on Startup India Portal
steps.description: Create your founder profile and startup profile on startupindia.gov.in.
details: Go to startupindia.gov.in
Click 'Register' → choose 'Startup'
Sign up with your Aadhaar-linked mobile number or email
Complete your founder profile with personal details
Fill startup details: entity name, CIN/LLPIN, date of incorporation, address
tips: Use the mobile number linked to your Aadhaar for smooth OTP verification
If Aadhaar is not linked to mobile, use email-based registration

steps.title: Fill the Recognition Application
steps.description: Complete the DPIIT recognition form with entity details, innovation description, and upload documents.
details: Stage of startup: select from Ideation / Validation / Early Traction / Scaling
Sector: select primary sector (15+ options available)
Brief description of innovative nature: explain your product/technology innovation (200-500 words)
Select applicable recognition category: Product innovation / Service innovation / Social impact / IP-based
Upload Certificate of Incorporation (PDF, < 2MB)
Optional: Upload pitch deck or product demo video (helps reviewers understand innovation)
Self-certify the declarations: entity age, turnover, innovation, no restructuring
documents_needed: Optional: Pitch deck (PDF), Letters of Support, Awards
tips: Write the innovation description clearly — 'we built an app' is not innovation; explain what problem you solve uniquely
Include keywords: 'technology-driven', 'scalable', 'novel approach', 'improvement over existing solutions'
For biotech/deep-tech: mention any patents filed, lab validations, or prototypes developed
The form has changed several times — screenshots from 2021 articles may be outdated

steps.title: Submit and Get DPIIT Number
steps.description: Submit your application. For most startups, recognition is instant or within 2-5 working days via automated review.
details: Click 'Submit Application' — you'll get an Application Reference Number immediately
DPIIT automated system reviews: ~80% of applications are auto-approved within 48-72 hours
If manual review required (complex sectors or insufficient description): 5-10 working days
You'll receive your DPIIT Recognition Certificate by email — download and save it
Your DPIIT Number format: DIPP######### (e.g., DIPP12345678)
tips: Save your DPIIT certificate PDF permanently — you'll need it for every scheme application
If rejected, you get specific reasons and can re-apply with corrections
Check application status at startupindia.gov.in under 'My Dashboard'

steps.title: Apply for Angel Tax Exemption (Form 2)
steps.description: Immediately after getting DPIIT number, file Form 2 to exempt yourself from angel tax before your first investment.
details: Log back into Startup India portal → 'Tax Exemption' section
File Form 2: Declaration for exemption under Section 56(2)(viib)
Required: DPIIT recognition certificate, Annual return of company, Current balance sheet
This is self-certification — no income tax department approval required
Protection applies retroactively from date of incorporation (not just from filing date)
documents_needed: Annual report / balance sheet (even if nil)
tips: File Form 2 BEFORE receiving your first external investment — once money is in, retroactive exemption has legal uncertainty
Angel tax exemption applies to investments up to ₹25 crore per year
File fresh Form 2 every year to maintain the exemption

steps.title: Apply for Section 80-IAC Tax Exemption
steps.description: After 3 years of operation, apply for the income tax holiday — 100% profit exemption for 3 consecutive years.
details: Eligibility: DPIIT-recognised startup with at least 3 years of filed IT returns
Apply at incometax.gov.in via e-filing portal → 'Startup' section
Application reviewed by Inter-Ministerial Board (IMB) — DPIIT, MSME, DBT representatives
IMB may call for a presentation if they need clarification on innovation
Approval takes 3-6 months but is retroactive to the year of incorporation
Once approved: choose any 3 consecutive years out of first 10 years for 100% profit deduction
documents_needed: CA certificate on innovation and IP
tips: Apply once you start generating profits — there's no point applying when losses are making PAT zero
Many startups wait until Series A to apply — VCs can help navigate the IMB process
80-IAC does not require profitability in all 3 years — just deduction claimed in profitable years

steps.title: Leverage Your DPIIT Status
steps.description: Now that you're DPIIT-recognised, activate all the benefits systematically.
details: GeM Registration: Register as seller on gem.gov.in using your DPIIT number — access Startup Runway
Fast-track patents: File patent at ipindia.gov.in — mention DPIIT number for 80% fee rebate
Labour law self-certification: Use Shram Suvidha portal for self-certification of 9 labour laws
Startup India Learning Program: Access free courses at startupindia.gov.in/learning
SISFS Seed Fund: Apply at seedfund.startupindia.gov.in if < 2 years old and pre-revenue
Credit Guarantee: Once you have bank loans, apply for CGSS (NCGTC) for guarantee cover
tips: Most schemes auto-verify your DPIIT status via PAN linking — keep PAN consistent across applications
Register on GeM within 1 week of DPIIT recognition — B2G sales can start immediately
Download the 'Startup India' mobile app to track your recognition status and benefits

faqs.q: I'm a solo founder at idea stage — can I apply for DPIIT recognition?
faqs.a: Yes! You need a registered entity (Pvt Ltd/LLP) but there's no revenue or team-size requirement. Many founders apply at the idea stage. Just ensure you have an incorporated entity.

faqs.q: My startup is more than 5 years old — can I still apply?
faqs.a: Yes, up to 10 years from incorporation. But hurry — some benefits (like SISFS seed fund) are only for startups < 2 years old.

faqs.q: Can an LLP get DPIIT recognition?
faqs.a: Yes! LLPs are eligible. However, if you plan to raise VC funding, consider converting to Private Limited Company — LLPs have FEMA complications with foreign investment.

faqs.q: What happens if my DPIIT application is rejected?
faqs.a: You receive specific rejection reasons and can reapply with corrections. Most rejections are due to vague innovation descriptions. Rewrite with specific technology and novelty claims.

faqs.q: Do I need a CA or lawyer to apply?
faqs.a: No! The application is entirely self-service on the Startup India portal. Most founders complete it in under 2 hours. The hardest part is writing the innovation description.
